cases.title: Haiti SEA scandal — staff used sex workers during 2010 earthquake response
cases.summary: The UK Charity Commission inquiry (concluded June 2019) found Oxfam GB mishandled allegations that senior aid workers, including the country director, used Haitian sex workers (some allegedly under 18) during the 2010 earthquake humanitarian response. Internal Oxfam investigation in 2011 had been kept confidential. The UK Government suspended funding and Oxfam was barred from bidding for new DFID/FCDO funding for two years. CEO Mark Goldring resigned. The case triggered sector-wide PSEA reforms.

cases.title: Resettlement fraud — refugees coerced into paying bribes up to $7,500 for relocation
cases.summary: Reuters investigation (Oct 2017) documented that UNHCR Kenya staff and intermediaries demanded bribes from Somali, Congolese, Sudanese and Ethiopian refugees in exchange for resettlement to the United States, Canada and Europe. Tens of thousands of dollars were paid; victims who refused were allegedly excluded from resettlement processing. UNHCR's Inspector General opened an investigation; multiple staff were terminated. The case drew condemnation from US Senate Foreign Relations Committee.

cases.title: Yemen — Houthi authorities diverted WFP food aid in Sana'a
cases.summary: AP and Reuters reporting (2018-2019), supported by WFP's own internal investigation, found that food aid was being systematically diverted by Houthi authorities controlling Sana'a, with biometric registration manipulated to misallocate aid. WFP partially suspended operations in Houthi-controlled areas in June 2019 until biometric monitoring was accepted. The case is one of the largest documented diversions of humanitarian food aid.
cases.amount: Hundreds of millions in aid affected

cases.title: DRC Ebola response — 83 women alleged sexual exploitation including by WHO staff
cases.summary: Joint investigation by The New Humanitarian and Thomson Reuters Foundation (Sep 2020) documented 51 women alleging sexual abuse by aid workers — later expanded to 83 — during the 2018-2020 Ebola response in eastern DRC. Independent Commission led by Aïchatou Mindaoudou (reported Sep 2021) confirmed widespread abuse and named WHO staff among perpetrators. Director-General Tedros Ghebreyesus apologized; WHO terminated multiple contracts and announced sweeping reforms.

cases.title: Senior staff sexual harassment — mishandled complaints inquiry
cases.summary: UK Charity Commission inquiry (concluded March 2020) found that Save the Children UK failed to properly handle multiple complaints of inappropriate behaviour by senior male executives, including former CEO Justin Forsyth and former director of policy Brendan Cox (2011-2015). Both had left the charity before the inquiry. The Commission found governance and HR failings; required improvements to safeguarding and complaints handling. UK government temporarily suspended new funding pending reforms.

cases.title: West Africa Ebola response — $6M fraud across Sierra Leone, Guinea, Liberia
cases.summary: IFRC announced (Nov 2017) it had uncovered approximately $6 million in fraud during the 2014-2016 Ebola response, including inflated customs bills (Sierra Leone, ~$2.13M), payroll fraud (Liberia, ~$2.7M) and overstated supplier costs (Guinea, ~$1M). IFRC pursued legal action; staff were dismissed; corrective measures put in place. The case prompted a sector-wide review of emergency procurement controls.

cases.title: Iraq Oil-for-Food kickbacks — Volcker independent inquiry
cases.summary: Independent Inquiry Committee (the 'Volcker Committee', report Oct 2005) found that Saddam Hussein's regime extracted ~$1.8 billion in kickbacks from over 2,200 companies via the UN Oil-for-Food Programme (1996-2003). UN Under-Secretary-General Benon Sevan was implicated for receiving payments; UN Secretariat oversight failures documented; some 270 companies investigated by national authorities. One of the largest documented UN procurement scandals.

cases.title: Leaked ethics report — Commissioner-General Krähenbühl resigned amid allegations
cases.summary: Internal UNRWA ethics office report (leaked to AFP, July 2019) alleged misconduct by senior management including Commissioner-General Pierre Krähenbühl: nepotism, abuse of authority, retaliation against whistleblowers, and creation of an inappropriate inner circle. Switzerland and the Netherlands suspended funding. Krähenbühl resigned in November 2019 following an OIOS investigation. UNRWA implemented governance reforms.

cases.title: Central African Republic — peacekeeper child sexual abuse allegations
cases.summary: Beginning 2014, multiple credible allegations emerged that UN peacekeepers (and earlier French Sangaris troops) sexually abused children in the Central African Republic, some as young as nine. UN OIOS opened multiple investigations; an independent panel led by Marie Deschamps found 'gross institutional failure' in handling allegations. Several troop-contributing countries repatriated contingents. The case spurred reform of UN PSEA mechanisms and 'paternity claims' policy.

cases.title: Afghanistan reconstruction — $69M settlement for inflated overhead
cases.summary: U.S. Department of Justice settlement (2015) with Louis Berger Group, a USAID contractor on Afghanistan reconstruction projects, for $69 million. The company admitted defrauding the U.S. government by inflating overhead rates billed under cost-reimbursement contracts (2003-2007). Two former executives pleaded guilty. Followed by a separate $17.1M False Claims Act settlement in 2010. Documented across multiple SIGAR reports.

cases.org: The Global Fund (recipients in Mali, Mauritania, Djibouti, Zambia)
cases.title: Misappropriation of TB/HIV/Malaria grants — multiple country recipients
cases.summary: The Global Fund Inspector General reports (2010-2011) documented misappropriation of grant funds in Mali (~$22.6M questioned), Mauritania (~$4.1M), Djibouti (~$1.5M), and Zambia. Sweden temporarily suspended its Global Fund contribution following the disclosures. The cases triggered creation of a strengthened Inspector General office and tighter financial controls.

cases.title: 'Cash for Kim' — hard currency payments and unmonitored disbursements
cases.summary: Allegations raised by US Mission to the UN (Jan 2007) that UNDP made unmonitored hard-currency payments to the DPRK government, employed staff selected by the regime, and procured equipment of potential dual-use. UNDP suspended its DPRK programme. External independent review (June 2008) found significant control weaknesses but no evidence funds were diverted to the WMD programme.
cases.source: UN External Independent Investigative Review Panel

cases.title: Internal SEA report — 24 dismissed in 2017 over harassment and abuse
cases.summary: MSF International published its 2017 internal report (June 2018) disclosing 24 staff dismissals for sexual harassment, abuse or exploitation over the year and 146 reported incidents of inappropriate behaviour. The transparency disclosure was part of post-Oxfam-scandal sector reforms. MSF subsequently strengthened its reporting hotline and behaviour code.

cases.title: Syria cross-border aid — USAID suspended funding over procurement fraud allegations
cases.summary: USAID OIG investigation (announced 2016) alleged collusion and bid-rigging by GOAL staff and intermediaries handling cross-border humanitarian aid into Syria from Turkey. USAID and the EU suspended funding. GOAL conducted an independent review, dismissed staff, and overhauled controls; partial USAID funding was later restored. Total questioned costs disputed; case widely reported in Devex and IRIN/The New Humanitarian.

cases.org: Tigray People's Liberation Front / WFP Ethiopia
cases.title: Northern Ethiopia — large-scale diversion of food aid documented by donors
cases.summary: USAID and WFP independently confirmed (May 2023) widespread diversion of food aid in northern Ethiopia (Tigray, Amhara, Afar). USAID and WFP suspended food aid to Ethiopia for several months pending reforms; investigation found military and government actors involved. Reuters and AP investigations documented systematic diversion. One of the largest documented humanitarian aid diversions in recent years.

cases.org: Various firms — World Bank Debarment List
cases.title: World Bank INT — over 200 firms currently debarred from Bank-financed projects
cases.summary: The World Bank Integrity Vice Presidency (INT) maintains a public list of firms and individuals barred from participating in World Bank Group-financed projects following findings of fraud, corruption, collusion, coercion or obstruction. The list includes companies from over 60 countries and is cross-debarred with AfDB, ADB, EBRD and IADB under the Mutual Enforcement Agreement.

cases.title: Pakistan polio eradication — $4.2M questioned in USAID OIG audit
cases.summary: USAID OIG audit (2013) of polio eradication funds channeled through partners in Pakistan questioned approximately $4.2 million in unsupported and unallowable costs. Issues included weak documentation, ineligible expenses, and procurement irregularities. Corrective action plans implemented; some recovery secured.

cases.title: Ebola procurement irregularities — internal audit findings
cases.summary: UNICEF Office of Internal Audit and Investigations (2015-2016) reported procurement and supply chain irregularities in the Ebola emergency response in Liberia, including inadequate vendor due diligence and weak goods-receipt controls. Corrective measures including procurement training and vendor verification implemented.

cases.title: Misconduct Disclosure Scheme — sector-wide effort to prevent re-hiring of abusers
cases.summary: Following the Oxfam Haiti scandal, IRC, Save the Children, Mercy Corps, World Vision and other major NGOs participate in the Misconduct Disclosure Scheme (administered by SCHR), which by 2024 had screened tens of thousands of references and prevented hundreds of confirmed abusers from being re-hired across the sector. While not a 'case' of misconduct itself, the MDS data shows the underlying scale of substantiated abuse.

cases.title: Iraq reconstruction — multiple SIGIR / SIGAR documented contractor frauds
cases.summary: Special Inspector General for Iraq Reconstruction (SIGIR, 2004-2013) and successor SIGAR documented dozens of contractor fraud cases totaling hundreds of millions of dollars: inflated billing, ghost employees, procurement collusion. Notable cases include conviction of Robert Stein ($3.6M kickbacks, 2007), and multiple False Claims Act settlements. Total questioned costs across SIGIR's lifecycle: over $4 billion.

cases.title: USAID Northern Syria — World Vision suspended over Hamas-linked staff allegations (2022)
cases.summary: USAID Office of Inspector General investigation (announced 2022) reviewed allegations that a former World Vision employee had connections to a US-designated entity. World Vision cooperated with the review; some operations suspended pending outcome. Case widely covered by Devex.

cases.title: AfDB Integrity — sanctions list of 90+ firms and individuals
cases.summary: African Development Bank Integrity & Anti-Corruption Office maintains a public sanctions list of debarred firms and individuals (90+ as of 2024) for fraud, corruption and collusion in Bank-financed projects across Africa. List is cross-recognised under the Mutual Enforcement Agreement with World Bank, ADB, EBRD, IADB.

cases.org: Mozambique 'tuna bonds' — Credit Suisse and government officials
cases.title: Mozambique hidden-debt scandal — $2B in concealed loans
cases.summary: Investigation (2016-2021) into $2 billion in loans to Mozambican state-owned companies (Ematum, Proindicus, MAM) hidden from the IMF and World Bank, leading to debt default and loss of donor budget support. Credit Suisse paid $475M settlement (Oct 2021) for misleading investors and bribing officials; ex-Finance Minister Manuel Chang extradited to Mozambique; multiple convictions. Triggered freeze of bilateral aid by US, UK, EU.

cases.title: Save the Children / UNHCR Liberia — 'sex for aid' findings
cases.summary: Save the Children UK and UNHCR joint report (Feb 2002, 'Sexual Violence and Exploitation: The Experience of Refugee Children in Liberia, Guinea and Sierra Leone') documented widespread sex-for-aid practices by humanitarian workers across 40+ agencies in West African refugee camps. Triggered the UN Secretary-General's Bulletin (ST/SGB/2003/13) on PSEA — the foundational PSEA policy still in force today.

cases.title: Somalia — UN Monitoring Group documented food aid diversion to Al-Shabaab
cases.summary: UN Monitoring Group on Somalia and Eritrea report (2010) found that up to 50% of food aid in Somalia was being diverted by transporters, partner agencies, or armed groups including Al-Shabaab. WFP suspended operations in Al-Shabaab-controlled areas. Catalyst for the introduction of biometric beneficiary registration and third-party monitoring across humanitarian agencies in conflict zones.

cases.title: Cox's Bazar Rohingya response — multiple SEA allegations against aid workers
cases.summary: Multiple reports (The New Humanitarian 2018-2020, BBC Media Action) documented allegations of sexual exploitation by aid workers in Cox's Bazar Rohingya refugee camps, including agency drivers, partner staff, and translators. Inter-agency PSEA network in Bangladesh investigated; multiple staff dismissals. Coordinated response strengthened by IASC/RC office.

cases.title: VSO whistleblower fraud allegations — UK Charity Commission inquiry
cases.summary: UK Charity Commission opened a regulatory case (2022) into Voluntary Service Overseas (VSO) following whistleblower allegations of fraud and procurement irregularities in country offices. VSO commissioned independent review; corrective actions undertaken. Devex and Civil Society Media reported on the case.

cases.title: Damascus-based UN procurement — Reuters, BBC documented favouring regime-linked firms
cases.summary: Multiple investigations (Reuters 2016, BBC Panorama 2016, The Guardian) found that UN agencies (UNDP, UNICEF, WFP, FAO) operating from Damascus contracted hundreds of millions of dollars to companies owned by individuals on EU/US sanctions lists or close to the Assad regime. UN responded that procurement followed exigency rules. Case sparked debate over neutrality of UN procurement in conflict zones.

cases.org: Multiple firms — IADB Office of Institutional Integrity sanctions
cases.title: IADB OII — sanctions list of 130+ firms and individuals
cases.summary: Inter-American Development Bank Office of Institutional Integrity (OII) maintains a public sanctions list of firms and individuals (130+ as of 2024) found to have engaged in fraudulent or corrupt practices in IADB-financed activities across Latin America and the Caribbean. Cross-debarred under Mutual Enforcement Agreement.

cases.org: Multiple firms — ADB Office of Anticorruption sanctions
cases.title: ADB OAI — sanctions list of 270+ firms and individuals
cases.summary: Asian Development Bank Office of Anticorruption and Integrity (OAI) maintains a public sanctions list of firms and individuals (270+ as of 2024) for fraud, corruption, collusion or coercion in ADB-financed projects across Asia and the Pacific. Cross-debarred under Mutual Enforcement Agreement.

cases.title: Sudan war response — competing transparency reporting on aid coordination failures
cases.summary: Multiple agencies including WHO, UNICEF and OCHA criticised by donors and humanitarian researchers (2023-2024) for coordination failures and limited transparency in the Sudan war response. The New Humanitarian, Devex and Foreign Policy reporting documented blocked-aid-corridor disputes and uncertainty over diversion in Darfur. Investigations ongoing.

cases.title: Ukraine response — USAID OIG flagged fraud risks in $24B+ in U.S. assistance
cases.summary: USAID OIG (2023-2024 reports) identified significant fraud risks across the $24+ billion in U.S. humanitarian and development assistance to Ukraine since the Russian invasion. Multiple investigations underway into specific implementing partners, procurement contracts, and cash-assistance programmes. Devex, Reuters, and OCCRP reporting.

cases.title: DR Congo — over 100 substantiated SEA allegations against MONUSCO personnel
cases.summary: UN OIOS and Conduct and Discipline Service data (cumulative through 2024) record more than 100 substantiated sexual exploitation and abuse allegations involving MONUSCO peacekeepers and civilian staff over the mission's duration. Multiple troop-contributing countries have repatriated personnel. UN paid paternity claims in dozens of cases.

cases.org: Afghanistan reconstruction — multiple SIGAR documented contractors
cases.title: SIGAR cumulative findings — $19B+ in waste, fraud, abuse identified
cases.summary: Special Inspector General for Afghanistan Reconstruction (SIGAR) cumulative reports (2008-2024) document at least $19 billion in waste, fraud and abuse across $146 billion in U.S. reconstruction funding. Hundreds of cases include ghost projects, ghost soldiers, contractor overbilling, falsified deliverables. Final SIGAR Lessons Learned reports published 2021-2023.

cases.title: UK Charity Commission case on safeguarding governance
cases.summary: UK Charity Commission engaged with Plan International UK (post-2018 sector-wide review) on safeguarding governance after concerns over reporting culture. Plan International published its 2017 disclosure of substantiated SEA cases as part of sector-wide transparency. Improvements implemented; case closed without formal inquiry.

organizations.name: Oxfam Great Britain
organizations.description: British registered charity, member of the global Oxfam confederation. Subject of major 2018 Haiti SEA scandal that triggered UK Charity Commission inquiry, suspension of UK government funding, and sector-wide PSEA reforms.

organizations.name: UNHCR
organizations.description: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees. Documented 2017 resettlement bribery case in Kenya operations exposed by Reuters investigation.

organizations.name: World Food Programme
organizations.description: World's largest humanitarian organization addressing hunger. Has documented diversion cases in Yemen (2018-19, Houthis), Somalia (2010, Al-Shabaab), and Ethiopia (2023, Tigray).

organizations.name: World Health Organization
organizations.description: UN specialized agency for international public health. 2018-2020 DRC Ebola response SEA scandal involved WHO staff (independent commission report 2021); ongoing scrutiny of Sudan war response.

organizations.name: Save the Children UK
organizations.description: British member of Save the Children International. UK Charity Commission inquiry (2020) found mishandling of harassment complaints against senior executives.
organizations.individuals: Justin Forsyth, Brendan Cox (departed before inquiry)

organizations.name: International Federation of Red Cross & Red Crescent
organizations.description: Global Red Cross and Red Crescent network. Disclosed (2017) approximately $6M in fraud during 2014-2016 West Africa Ebola response.

organizations.name: UN Oil-for-Food Programme
organizations.description: UN-administered programme (1996-2003) allowing Iraq to sell oil for humanitarian goods. Volcker independent inquiry (2005) documented $1.8B in kickbacks; one of the largest UN scandals.

organizations.name: UNRWA
organizations.description: UN Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees. 2019 leaked ethics report; donor funding suspensions; Commissioner-General Krähenbühl resignation.

organizations.name: UN Peacekeeping (MINUSCA)
organizations.description: UN Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in CAR. Documented child SEA allegations from 2014 onwards; Deschamps independent panel found 'gross institutional failure'.

organizations.name: Louis Berger Group
organizations.description: American engineering and consulting firm; major USAID Afghanistan reconstruction contractor. $69M DOJ settlement (2015) for inflated overhead billing; two executives pleaded guilty.

organizations.name: The Global Fund (recipients)
organizations.description: Multilateral fund for HIV, TB, malaria. 2010-2011 misappropriation cases by recipient governments (Mali, Mauritania, Djibouti, Zambia) led to Sweden's funding suspension and Inspector General reforms.

organizations.name: UNDP North Korea
organizations.description: UN Development Programme operations in DPRK; suspended in 2007 after US allegations of unmonitored hard-currency payments and procurement of dual-use equipment. External independent review (2008) found significant control weaknesses.

organizations.name: Médecins Sans Frontières
organizations.description: Doctors Without Borders. Disclosed (June 2018) 24 staff dismissals for harassment/abuse over 2017 as part of post-Oxfam transparency reforms.

organizations.name: GOAL Ireland
organizations.description: Irish humanitarian organization. USAID/EU funding suspended in 2016 over Syria cross-border procurement fraud allegations; partial USAID funding later restored after corrective action.

organizations.name: WFP Ethiopia / TPLF (diversion)
organizations.description: Northern Ethiopia operations. May 2023 USAID/WFP confirmed widespread food aid diversion; aid suspended for several months.

organizations.name: World Bank — debarred firms (collective)
organizations.description: World Bank Integrity Vice Presidency public list of 200+ firms and individuals barred from Bank-financed projects.

organizations.name: GAVI Alliance
organizations.description: Global vaccine alliance. 2013 USAID OIG audit of polio funds in Pakistan questioned $4.2M.

organizations.name: UNICEF
organizations.description: UN Children's Fund. Multiple OIAI audit findings over the years; 2015-2016 Liberia Ebola procurement issues among them.

organizations.name: International Rescue Committee
organizations.description: Major international humanitarian NGO; participates in the Misconduct Disclosure Scheme, the post-Oxfam sector-wide effort to prevent re-hire of confirmed abusers.

organizations.name: USAID Iraq contractors (collective)
organizations.description: Various contractors implicated in SIGIR/SIGAR-documented Iraq reconstruction fraud cases ($4B+ questioned).

organizations.name: World Vision
organizations.description: Christian humanitarian organization. 2022 USAID OIG investigation reviewed counter-terrorism vetting in Gaza programmes.

organizations.name: African Development Bank — sanctions list
organizations.description: AfDB Integrity & Anti-Corruption Office sanctions list (90+ firms/individuals).

organizations.name: Mozambique tuna bonds — Credit Suisse / govt
organizations.description: $2B in hidden state-backed debt; Credit Suisse $475M settlement; ex-Finance Minister Manuel Chang convicted.

organizations.name: UNHCR / Save the Children — Liberia 2002 report
organizations.description: Foundational PSEA report (Feb 2002) documenting sex-for-aid practices across 40+ humanitarian agencies in West African refugee camps. Triggered the UN PSEA policy.

organizations.name: Cox's Bazar Rohingya response (collective)
organizations.description: Multi-agency Rohingya refugee response in Cox's Bazar; documented SEA allegations against drivers, partner staff, and translators across multiple agencies.

organizations.name: Voluntary Service Overseas (VSO)
organizations.description: British international development charity. UK Charity Commission engagement in 2022 following whistleblower fraud allegations.

organizations.name: Damascus UN procurement (collective)
organizations.description: UNDP / UNICEF / WFP / FAO Damascus operations; documented contracting with regime-linked or sanctioned firms (Reuters, BBC, Guardian 2016).

organizations.name: IADB — sanctions list
organizations.description: Inter-American Development Bank Office of Institutional Integrity sanctions list (130+ firms/individuals).

organizations.name: ADB — sanctions list
organizations.description: Asian Development Bank Office of Anticorruption and Integrity sanctions list (270+ firms/individuals).

organizations.name: USAID Ukraine response (collective)
organizations.description: $24B+ in U.S. assistance to Ukraine since 2022; USAID OIG flagged significant fraud risks; multiple investigations ongoing.

organizations.name: UN MONUSCO
organizations.description: UN Stabilization Mission in DR Congo. 100+ substantiated SEA allegations cumulatively per UN data.

organizations.name: Afghanistan reconstruction (SIGAR)
organizations.description: SIGAR cumulative findings: $19B+ in waste, fraud, abuse across $146B in U.S. Afghanistan reconstruction funding.

organizations.name: Plan International UK
organizations.description: Children's NGO; UK Charity Commission engagement in 2018 on safeguarding governance.
